zotero.abstractNote: Deep learning models have achieved state-of-the-art performance in medical image analysis but are often criticized for their lack of interpretability. While post-hoc explanation methods exist, they can be misleading and do not guarantee faithfulness to the model's decision-making process. In this work, we propose an inherently interpretable multi-label classification framework that leverages class-specific counterfactuals. Our approach learns a set of class-specific prototypes and generates counterfactual explanations by identifying the minimal changes required to flip the classification outcome. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on multi-label chest X-ray classification, showing that it provides faithful and intuitive explanations while maintaining competitive classification performance.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the Medical Imaging with Deep Learning 2023
